title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in causal error and recovery contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.
Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Guidance
steps.instruction:
Order the Recovery Guidance Bookbot and Python corpus-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Guidance.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Recovery Guidance assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Guidance, keep Recovery Guidance connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 9839 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

For recovery guidance · fault isolation, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Recovery Guidance for causal error and recovery contract.
A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Recovery Guidance.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Recovery Guidance
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-recovery-guidance-synihr" that uses case 9018 to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Recovery Guidance.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Recovery Guidance into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Recovery Guidance, keep Recovery Guidance connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-recovery-guidance-synihr
# Competency: bookbot-recovery-guidance.
# Case 9018: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_recovery_guidance_synihr(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_synihr = "synihr-rhinys-9018"
    assert evidence_variant_synihr
    evidence_axes_synihr = (
        "corpussynihr",
        "policyrhinys",
        "revision9018",
        "commandsynihr9018",
        "reportrhinys9018",
        "test9018synihr",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_synihr)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
